name: alpha_satellite_higher_order_repeat
description: Architectural archetype: human alpha-satellite (AS, 'alphoid') DNA. The fundamental unit is a 171 bp AT-rich monomer (CENP-B-box-bearing in ~50% of monomers). Monomers are organised into chromosome-specific higher-order repeats (HORs) typically 2-34 monomers (~340-5800 bp) that themselves tandemly repeat across the active alpha-satellite (alphaSat-HOR) centromeric array. Flanking dimeric/monomeric alphaSat lacks HOR organisation.

architecture.internal_structure: 171 bp AT-rich monomer (CENP-B box CTTCGTTGGAAACGGGA in B-monomers) -> chromosome-specific HOR (2-34 monomers, fixed monomer order) -> tandem HOR array (5-30 Mb per centromere). Active HOR sequence drift ~5% between flanking HORs and ~0.1% within an array. Boundary HORs less regular; flanking monomeric alpha-sat highly diverged (15-25% per monomer).
